perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed during the ON Semiconductor Industry Analyst Conference to be held by invitation only on April 18-20, 2011 , in Scottsdale, Arizona, hereunder may only be used for the following purpose: For evaluation and discussion of market tr ends and data refinement in the areas of ON Semiconductor’s focus market segments. (hereinafter “Authorized Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text:
Confidential Information disclosed during the ON Semiconductor Industry Analyst Conference to be held by invitation only on April 18-20, 2011 , in Scottsdale, Arizona, hereunder may only be used for the following purpose:
Recipient agrees it will not, in any form, export, re-export, resell, ship or divert or cause to be exported, re-exported, re-sold, shipped or diverted, directly or indirectly, any product or technical dat a furnished hereunder, or the direct product of such technical data, to any country for which the United States Government requires an export license, or other approval, with out first obtaining such license or approval. (hereinafter “Authorized Purpose”).
perturbation.explanation: The definition of the Authorized Purpose and the export regulations are now intertwined. This makes it hard to understand the actual definition. The definition should be clear.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 CFR § 734.2 defines export control regulations. By mixing the export statement with the definition of authorized purpose, it is less clear when and if export controls apply to the information shared, potentially violating requirements for clear communication of export restrictions.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: EAR | Bureau of Industry and Security An official website of the United States government Here's how you know Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. Skip to main content Export Administration Regulations (EAR) This tool helps you navigate the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774. Commerce Control List (CCL) The Commerce Control List (CCL) (Supplement No. 1 to Part 774 of the EAR) is divided into ten broad categories, and each category is further subdivided into five product groups. Additionally, you may explore our interactive tool . Category 0 - Nuclear Materials Facilities & Equipment [and Miscellaneous Items] Category 1 - Special Materials and Related Equipment, Chemicals, “Microorganisms,” and “Toxins” Category 2 - Materials Processing Category 3 - Electronics Category 4 - Computers Category 5 Part 1 - Telecommunications Category 5 Part 2 - Information Security Category 6 - Sensors and Lasers Category 7 - Navigation and Avionics Category 8 - Marine Category 9 - Aerospace and Propulsion Legal limitations of the tools on the BIS website The tools on the BIS website are not intended to replace the official rules found in the Code of Federal Regulations , nor are they a primer on how to get started. For that introductory guidance, please visit our Learn and Support page. The tools are an editorial compilation of material and not an official legal edition of the Code of Federal Regulations or the Federal Register . We have made every effort to ensure the material presented in these tools are accurate, but if you are relying on them for legal research you should consult the official editions of those sources to confirm your findings. Nothing in these tools binds BIS or creates any rights, benefits, or defenses, substantive or procedural, that are enforceable by any party in any manner. If you find any errors, please email us.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by intertwining the definition of "Authorized Purpose" with export control regulations. This makes the definition unclear and potentially undermines the requirements for clear communication of export restrictions, which is relevant under 15 CFR § 734.2, as explained in the Law Explanation. The official website of the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) provides tools to navigate the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774, highlighting the importance of understanding and adhering to export control requirements, further supporting that the modification introduces a legal contradiction.
